supersedes.scope: Only the scheduling-sensitive fingerprint command and its v1 baseline counts and hash are superseded.
preserved: The original non-keep-going invocation remains BASELINE_RED evidence and is not a comparator.
The v1 task boundary, no-ADR disposition, absolute-green non-clippy gates, and forbidden dispositions remain in force.
No implementation, specification, readiness, traceability, graph, authority, dependency, runtime, or product scope is changed.

trigger.new_fact: Without Cargo --keep-going, denied-warning failures cancel unscheduled targets, so the emitted diagnostic subset changes with scheduling and cannot be a repeatable baseline fingerprint.
trigger.affected_step_stopped: R-051 remains below DEV_VERIFIED until the owner records two internally identical final-head canonical runs under this v2 recipe and satisfies every differential and absolute-green gate.

canonicalization_recipe.input: Every Cargo JSON line whose reason is compiler-message and whose message.code is non-null.
tuple: the file_name of the first primary span, or <no-primary>
canonicalization_recipe.ordering_and_grouping: Sort tuple objects lexicographically by code, file, and message; group exact adjacent tuples; emit one object per group with exact multiplicity.
canonicalization_recipe.byte_contract: UTF-8 jq default pretty JSON for the single array, two-space indentation, comma-space object separators as emitted by jq-1.7.1-apple, and exactly one trailing LF. Do not use -c, -S, color, or raw-output flags.
canonicalization_recipe.excluded: Source coordinates, rendered ANSI text, target scheduling/order, diagnostics with a null code, and raw JSONL ordering.
canonicalization_recipe.fable_precision_correction_applied: The script and serialization recipe are now embedded and digest-pinned. Applying this exact recipe to both Fable raw runs reproduces the PM canonical SHA-256.

pass_rule: The base and final runs use identical toolchain transcript, Cargo.lock, argv, jq recipe, and allowlisted-environment digests.
Two base canonical arrays and two final canonical arrays are each internally byte-identical.
The final non-owned-path fingerprint multiset is exactly equal to the baseline non-owned-path multiset.
For every owned-path fingerprint, final multiplicity is less than or equal to baseline multiplicity.
No final fingerprint absent from the baseline is allowed in either partition.
The R-051 diff adds no allow, expect, cap-lints, lint-level, or lint-configuration escape.
The expected exit code remains 101 because unrelated baseline diagnostics remain; the exit is never called a green result.

boundary_disposition.adr_rationale: This is a verification-truth correction for a pre-existing repository condition. Authority, dependencies, writes, serialized contracts, runtime behavior, and product scope are unchanged.

standing_fable_advisory.basis: Fable independently ran the pinned base command twice, confirmed the exact 200/460 and 10/19 plus 190/441 multisets and the scheduling-sensitivity diagnosis, and endorsed --keep-going as the smallest truthful correction.
standing_fable_advisory.mandatory_precision_applied: The exact canonicalizer, script digest, jq identity, field order, byte serialization, invocation, and trailing newline are now pinned; both Fable raw runs reproduce canonical SHA-256 3288d73619aea821118069484194941edd5f83484a0baa2297be633f1c89fbd3 under that recipe.
standing_fable_advisory.operational_note: The provider updated its own non-repository Claude plan file despite the read-only prompt. No project or dependency repository file was authored or changed; the plan-file write is not accepted as evidence and the advisory remains non-authoritative.

fresh_review_requirement.implementation_review_cycle: fresh independent Opus session after the final R-051 DEV_VERIFIED head
fresh_review_requirement.required_reproduction: Run the exact pinned command twice on base and twice on final, apply the digest-pinned recipe, verify both internal equality checks and every differential rule, then reproduce every absolute-green gate with clean pre/post state and repository_authorship=false.

pm_decision.basis: The keep-going baseline is repeatable across two PM and two independent Fable runs, Fable's one precision correction is applied by the digest-pinned canonical recipe, v1 is explicitly superseded, no ADR trigger changes, and final implementation still requires owner verification, fresh Opus, and a separate PM audit.

next_action: Bind v2 to the same R-051 owner, capture two final-head canonical runs and the exact delta, finish all absolute-green gates, then run standing Fable task review, fresh independent Opus review, and main-PM acceptance.
